name: New England Clam Chowder from Fred Goslin
recipeInstructions:
This chowder is not as good as the one served at Jay's Diner in Rochester, NY, but it's close...
1. Place bacon in a 2-quart casserole and microwave on high (100%) until crisp, about 3 to 4 minutes. Add the reserved clam juice, potatoes, and onions. Cover and microwave on high (100%) until potatoes are tender, about 8 to 10 minutes, stirring after half the cooking time.
2. Blend in the flour, stir in milk, salt, and pepper. Microwave on high (100%) until thickened, about 4 to 7 minutes, stirring 2 to 3 times during the cooking time.
3. Add the clams and continue microwaving on medium-high (70%), stirring occasionally, until heated through, approximately 4 to 5 minutes.
